steps.title: Look for both traits
steps.content: The claim has two parts: Lady Chiltern is perceived as extremely virtuous and as unforgiving. The best quotation must show both moral perfection and harshness, not just one of those qualities.

steps.title: Match virtue with severity
steps.content: A shows high principles but not unforgivingness. B presents a cynical view of morality, not a direct description of mercy or harshness in Lady Chiltern. D describes her as sensible, which does not address either required trait. The remaining quotation combines moral goodness with being pitiless, cold, stern, and without mercy. The answer is C.
